result:
Done. Map 3 "The Summit" shipped to main.

## What changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `app/api/db/seed-map3/route.ts` | **New** — `POST /api/db/seed-map3` seeds 25 heaps (orders 51–75) with `map_id=3` |
| `app/api/db/migrate/route.ts` | Added Map 3 row: `(3, 'The Summit', 'volcano', 3, ...)` |
| `components/MapView.tsx` | Added `orange` accent set + `accentFor()` now maps `volcano → orange` |
| `app/(app)/map/page.tsx` | Fixed empty-table fallback to label Map 3 correctly |

## How it works
- **Unlock chain** is global by heap `order`, so heaps 51–75 auto-unlock once Map 2 (heaps 26–50) is complete — no extra logic needed.
- **MapSwitcher / `/api/maps` / map page** auto-discover Map 3 from the DB; orange theme flows through automatically (Map 1 emerald, Map 2 violet, Map 3 orange).
- Tailwind picks up the orange classes since they're literal strings in `MapView.tsx`.

## To activate on a deployment
Run the two endpoints with the migration secret:
1. `POST /api/db/migrate` — adds the Map 3 row
2. `POST /api/db/seed-map3` — seeds the 25 heaps (idempotent; skips any order ≥51 already present)

All 25 heaps have real Bulgarian translations (Cyrillic + Latin transliteration), 5 words each, themed per your spec. `npm run build` passes; the 24 lint errors are all pre-existing in unrelated files (PWAProvider, etc.).
